approach: Fail-closed order-42 corpus acquisition through a new mirror path, plus complete-adjacency parser hardening

strategy.mechanism: A frozen byte preflight gates two exact evaluators, full upper-triangle agreement across two custom parsers and NetworkX, nauty complementation, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: The Hugging Face resolve path can supply the frozen corpus bytes, and full adjacency fingerprints can eliminate the remaining parser-equivalence weakness.
discriminating_test: Attempt one bounded mirror download; require 47,888 bytes, 328 LF records, and the frozen SHA-256 before target work; require all three parsers to agree on complete adjacency.
summary: The new Hugging Face acquisition path failed DNS resolution with curl code 6 and left no corpus or HTTP headers. The final preflight returned code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false. The verifier was strengthened from order/edge/degree signatures to complete upper-triangle fingerprints. Its exhaustive order-7 regression passed on all 1,044 unlabeled classes and their complements, including exact evaluator agreement, semantic mutations, malformed-input rejection, and byte-exact nauty complementation. Zero order-42 records were checked and no Ramsey bound changed.
rationale: The source blocker remains, but complete fingerprints remove a concrete verification gap that coarse graph signatures could miss. The result is scoped research-software progress, not target-order mathematical evidence.
claims: Python, C, and NetworkX produced identical complete adjacency fingerprints for 2,088 order-7 source/complement records.
The two exact evaluators agreed, and local complement bytes matched nauty-complg throughout that suite.
The provisional K43 controls still pass, but publisher authentication remains false.
No order-42 graph was evaluated; the rigorous range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
next_steps: Do not retry ANU or Hugging Face curl through the unchanged command network.
Obtain unchanged McKay bytes through a genuinely new export path or a user-supplied file with retrieval provenance.
Run the authenticated-corpus command recorded in CHECKPOINT.md and stop at the first mismatch.
Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 only after the corpus gate passes.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-210542-55792e: mirror acquisition failed with curl code 6
independent_checker: Checker A uses Python, a Boolean matrix, and direct 5-subset enumeration. Checker B uses C, a separate parser, and recursive bitset clique enumeration. NetworkX independently decodes complete adjacency; nauty-complg checks complement bytes. None ran at order 42 because the source was absent.
transfer_insights: Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> coarse signatures may hide decoding errors -> complete fingerprints agreed across three parsers on the exhaustive order-7 suite.
Fail-closed provenance -> absent raw bytes must prevent mathematical evaluation -> downstream_checks_run remained false.

established_facts.claim: Complete adjacency fingerprints from three parser paths agree on every unlabeled order-7 graph class and complement.
established_facts.scope: 1,044 representatives and 1,044 complements at order 7 only

established_facts.claim: The maintained published range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Retry direct curl acquisition from ANU or Hugging Face in the unchanged command environment.
ruled_out.reason: Both hosts fail DNS resolution before any HTTP response.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A new network/export route or user-supplied raw file with provenance becomes available.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Start order-42 novelty or K43 repair search from the current packet.
ruled_out.scope: Any continuation lacking authenticated McKay and Springer bytes
ruled_out.reason: The source preflight stopped before every target-instance check.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: The McKay corpus passes the complete-fingerprint gate and the Springer seed then passes its raw-source gate.

open_leads.description: Authenticate the 328 McKay records and their complements.
open_leads.rationale: Every novelty and canonical-comparison claim depends on these controls.
open_leads.next_experiment: Place unchanged bytes with provenance at sources/r55_42some.g6 and run the exact CHECKPOINT.md command.

open_leads.description: Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 after the corpus passes.
open_leads.rationale: It is the strongest published repair seed, but the local transcription lacks publisher provenance.
open_leads.next_experiment: Retrieve the unchanged TXT, determine its color convention, and require exact violation identities under both checkers.

blocker: Neither tested command host resolves, browser retrieval cannot export the raw body, and no authenticated order-42 corpus exists locally.
reopen_condition: Provide or retrieve unchanged McKay bytes with provenance matching the frozen size, record count, and SHA-256 discriminator.
policy_flags: Repeated blocked strategy r55controls2026 without evidence satisfying its reopen condition.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: Can later R(5,5) computations use a fail-closed control gate that detects every adjacency-level parser disagreement?
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: The previous third-parser gate compared only order, edge count, and degrees; this version compares every adjacency bit and retains per-record fingerprint hashes.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: The unchanged authoritative corpus passes the final gate from a cold start and receives an independent external replay.

closest_prior_work.difference: McKay supplies the historical graphs; this artifact adds fail-closed multi-parser verification and claims no new graph or classification.

novelty_searches.source: Dynamic Survey DS1.18 and maintained corpus index
novelty_searches.query: current R(5,5) range and r55_42some corpus status
novelty_searches.finding: The exact value remains open, and the maintained corpus is not claimed to be a complete classification.

external_channel.acceptance_path: No contact or submission until authoritative raw-source replay succeeds.

independent_validations.validator: NetworkX 3.3 and nauty 2.8.8+ds-5 local exhaustive regression
independent_validations.result: Complete adjacency and byte-exact complement agreement on all 2,088 order-7 records

continuation.objective: Close the authoritative order-42 corpus control gate.
continuation.first_action: Supply sources/r55_42some.g6 through a genuinely new raw-file path, preserve retrieval metadata, and run the command in CHECKPOINT.md.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop at any provenance, byte, LF termination, record-count, full 861-bit parser fingerprint, forbidden-set, histogram, degree, complement, or canonical-label mismatch.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol was principal investigator. Two precomputed GPT-5.6 Terra memos were advisory; the relied experiment-verification memo was hash-verified, promoted byte-for-byte into docs/delegate-memos, and independently implemented and tested by Sol. Tools used: Python 3.12.3, C/GCC, nauty 2.8.8+ds-5, NetworkX 3.3, curl, SHA-256, Git diagnostics, the computational-researcher experiment harness, and web browsing. No new subagent, SAT solver, proof assistant, or cloud job was used.; orchestration: gpt-5.6-sol principal with gpt-5.6-terra delegates.
